response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive software development experience, including significant work with C and C++ (e.g., National Instruments, embedded systems), but his recent experience leans heavily toward Node.js, TypeScript, and other languages/platforms (e.g., AWS, React) rather than C++ for industrial automation or on-premises/cloud platform solutions. His lack of recent or deep C++ STL/object-oriented experience, along with no clear exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or message-broker systems, raises concerns about immediate fit. However, his strong problem-solving, architectural, and agile collaboration skills could make him a potential cultural fit with additional training.
strengths: Strong background in C/C++ (e.g., National Instruments, embedded systems) with hands-on low-latency and high-performance development experience.
Diverse technical expertise across languages/frameworks (e.g., Node.js, TypeScript, D, Java) and systems (e.g., Kubernetes, real-time data) demonstrating adaptability.
Proven track record in software architecture, mentorship (e.g., Refactoring Dojos, workshops), and agile practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD).
concerns: Limited recent or deep experience with C++ STL and modern C++ object-oriented principles required for the role (e.g., industrial automation, on-premises/cloud platforms).
No visible exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (e.g., IEC 62443) or message-broker/event-driven architectures (e.g., MQTT, RabbitMQ, Kafka) critical to the role.
Lacks direct experience with cloud platforms (e.g., Azure) or industrial protocols (e.g., Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA) mentioned as advantageous or core competencies.
